description: Trigger discrimination for GATHERING/eliciting requirements (this skill) vs WRITING the spec afterward (product-spec-writer), vs modeling the domain (domain-modeler), vs reconciling conflicting FACTS (source-of-truth-reconciler).

cases.prompt: The request is vague and stated as a solution, users disagree, and we don't really know the problem yet. Facilitate getting to clear requirements.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — vague, solution-first, conflicting input with no understood problem is the elicitation phase. No spec is being written and no domain model is being built.
cases.reason: Discovery, problem-vs-solution separation, and conflict surfacing before a spec exists is this skill's core scope.

cases.prompt: Requirements are understood and agreed. Produce the product spec: problem statement, goals and non-goals, user stories, acceptance criteria, and success metrics.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (reverse, within pack) — with requirements settled, authoring the structured specification document is product-spec-writer. Elicitation is done.
cases.reason: product-spec-writer turns an understood need into a spec. This skill produces the brief that feeds it; it does not write the spec.

cases.prompt: Map out the core domain concepts for this area — the entities, their attributes, and the relationships and invariants between them.
cases.reason: Modeling entities, relationships, and invariants is domain-modeler. Requirements elicitation is about the stakeholders' need, not the conceptual model of the domain.

cases.prompt: Two docs and the code disagree on what the current retention period actually is. Which one is authoritative?
cases.reason: Resolving which of several sources states the correct FACT is source-of-truth-reconciler. This skill reconciles stakeholder disagreement about what to BUILD, not contested facts.
